claim.observation: Volatility 3 windows.psscan output (Framework 2.27.0) lists a process with ImageFileName 'subject_srv.ex' at PID 6160, PPID 536, Offset(V) 0x3adf6080, 12 threads, SessionId 0, Wow64 True, CreateTime 2018-09-06 19:25:36.000000 UTC, and ExitTime N/A. It is the only process in this output whose Wow64 column reads True.
claim.interpretation: The image name 'subject_srv.ex' (truncated by psscan's fixed-width ImageFileName field) does not match a standard Windows system binary, and the process is a 32-bit (Wow64 True) process under SessionId 0 with 12 threads and no recorded exit time (ExitTime N/A) -- anomalous relative to every other process in this listing. This makes it the strongest candidate for follow-up (image path / on-disk location, loaded DLLs, network handles, and the identity of PPID 536). psscan alone does NOT establish malware, C2, exfiltration, or persistence; those claims would require additional evidence.
